title: Multi-tab assistant interface MVP (Claude Codex Copilot)
desc: Design a VS Code extension style multi-tab assistant interface as an integration reference for AppBot, focusing on provider adapters, shared event flow, and isolated sessions for Claude, Codex, and Copilot. Use when translating a multi-provider chat experience into AppBot or when porting only the orchestration layer, session model, and provider boundaries without cloning a private backend.
steps: Define the porting boundary first: reuse the orchestration and session model, not the private backend or UI chrome.
Use a three-pane layout only as a reference shape: provider tabs, conversation thread, and composer or status footer.
Keep provider sessions isolated per workspace and per provider so tab switches never leak context.
Use one canonical event schema for user message, assistant delta, tool call, tool result, and final response.
Keep provider-specific behavior inside adapters; the UI should only speak to the shared contract.
Support streaming parity, cancel, retry last turn, and regenerate response at the adapter boundary.
Inject workspace context explicitly and visibly, including active folder, selected files, and recent command history.
Gate tool actions through permissions and show progress events in the timeline instead of hiding them.
Surface rate limit, timeout, reconnect, and offline states as first-class UX states.
Persist compact session snapshots locally by workspace and provider so resume stays cheap and deterministic.
Deliver in phases: skeleton, adapters, permissions and resume, then polish and observability.
author: claude
